subject: Re: Warp Extracting Subtomograms Multiple Times
body:
A classic case of, "Have you tried turning it off and on again?".
Glad to hear that you managed to get to the bottom of it.
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK

On Sun, Oct 24, 2021 at 12:02 PM 3P1L <philipp.stawski@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> It appears that in Warp's metadata, several versions existed for some tomograms. I recall that it had crashed during the tomo CTF task.
>
> When resuming, I noticed that the already finished ones were shown as "outdated".
>
> Strange though that values didn't get replaced but duplicated. 🤔
>
> During the extraction, Warp then apparently treated each entry (as apparent from different CTF max res values) as separate tomograms and extracted 2x, 3x, ...
>
> Restarting the entire project in a new folder fixed it. 😅
>
> Thanks for your input!
>
> Philipp.
>
> On Monday, October 18, 2021 at 2:42:17 PM UTC+2 3P1L wrote:
>>
>> Yes. It is a strange multiplication effect ... I haven't quite figured out how this is possible ... funnily enough,
>>
>> the original file used to extract particles at bin8, doesn't cause this effect ...
>>
>> On Monday, October 18, 2021 at 1:36:43 PM UTC+2 lorenzo...@gmail.com wrote:
>>>
>>> Hi Phillip,
>>>
>>> Quite odd! From what I gather from the star files, It seems that all particles are duplicated once. From index 0 to 236 are duplicated EXACTLY (they appear again from 262 to 498). Those between 237 and 261 also appear again after 499, but with some parameters changed. Here's where they differ (False) versus where they are the same (True).
>>>
>>> rlnMicrographName          True
>>> rlnCoordinateX             True
>>> rlnCoordinateY             True
>>> rlnCoordinateZ             True
>>> rlnImageName              False
>>> rlnCtfImage               False
>>> rlnAngleRot                True
>>> rlnAngleTilt               True
>>> rlnAnglePsi                True
>>> rlnMagnification           True
>>> rlnDetectorPixelSize       True
>>> rlnCtfMaxResolution       False
>>> rlnPixelSize               True
>>> rlnVoltage                 True
>>> rlnSphericalAberration     True
>>>
>>> For example, this is the last particle in the first and the second file:
>>> rlnImageName               ../tilts/subtomo/Position_3/Position_3_0000261_6.40A.mrc
>>> rlnCtfImage            ../tilts/subtomo/Position_3/Position_3_0000261_ctf_6.40A.mrc
>>> rlnCtfMaxResolution                                                             6.0
>>>
>>> rlnImageName               ../tilts/subtomo/210610_Lamela1_Position_35/210610_Lamela1_Position_35_0000261_6.40A.mrc
>>> rlnCtfImage            ../tilts/subtomo/210610_Lamela1_Position_35/210610_Lamela1_Position_35_0000261_ctf_6.40A.mrc
>>> rlnCtfMaxResolution                                                                                             4.8
>>>
>>> As far as I can tell, anything before 262 is the uncorrupted one. Hope this helps!
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> Lorenzo
>>>
>>> On Mon, Oct 18, 2021 at 1:12 PM Pranav Shah <p.sha...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>>> This is an interesting pathology Phillip and I cannot say that I have
>>>> seen this problem before, but if I had to troubleshoot this problem I
>>>> would try the following things
>>>> 1. If you extract the full dataset multiple times, do the same
>>>> particles get extracted multiple times every time?
>>>> 2. What happens if you extract one and only one (random) particle?
>>>> 3. If 1 is true can you try 2 on atleaset on one of those particles?
>>>> Best,
>>>> Pranav
>>>> --
>>>> Pranav Shah
>>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>>>
>>>> Division of Structural Biology,
>>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>>>> University of Oxford,
>>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>>>> UK
>>>>
>>>> On Mon, Oct 18, 2021 at 11:32 AM 3P1L <philipp...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>> >
>>>> > So ... I've still not found a solution to the problem. Warp keeps extracting particles from some tomograms 2x or 3x.
>>>> >
>>>> > On different machines, different versions 😳
>>>> >
>>>> > I've attached the input (bin4_test_reduced.star) and output (bin4_test_reduced_OUTPUT.star) of a minimal star file (one tomogram), where particles are always extracted 2x.
>>>> >
>>>> > Does anybody spot anything suspicious that could cause such a behavior?
>>>> >
>>>> > I get the same behavior if I use star files written by me (matlab; tom_toolbox) or by Relion (3.x).
>>>> >
>>>> > Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated! 🙇‍♂️
>>>> >
>>>> >
>>>> > Philipp.
>>>> >
>>>> > On Tuesday, October 5, 2021 at 4:34:47 PM UTC+2 3P1L wrote:
>>>> >>
>>>> >> Dear All,
>>>> >>
>>>> >> I've encountered an interesting behavior of Warp when reconstructing subtomograms:
>>>> >>
>>>> >> I go in with a particle list of ~15k particles (from Relion 3.1), out comes a list of ~23k particles.
>>>> >>
>>>> >> I guess this is one way to lift up that FSC curve 😜
>>>> >>
>>>> >> Apparently, for some of the tomograms particles are extracted 2x and 3x. 🤔 [didn't see an obvious pattern such as this would always happen to the 3rd, 6th, ... or 4th, 8th, ...  tomogram].
>>>> >>
>>>> >> The setup:
>>>> >>
>>>> >> Warp 1.0.9 on
>>>> >> Windows 10 Pro 64 Bit, Intel Xeon Gold 6230
>>>> >> 384 GB RAM, 6xRTX 2080 Ti
>>>> >>
>>>> >> Anybody ever seen this?
>>>> >>
>>>> >> [I'm currently testing if this happens, too, if I disable some of the GPUs; will report later]
>>>> >>
>>>> >> Thanks,
>>>> >>
>>>> >> Philipp.
>>>> >>
>>>> >>
>>>> > --
